World Class Spotlight-We had the opportunity to be among the first to watch the remake of Anaconda, and it was an absolute blast. From start to finish, the movie kept us laughing, thanks to the incredible chemistry and comedic timing of Jack Black, Paul Rudd, and the rest of the cast. The film perfectly balances humor, adventure, and over-the-top moments that make it wildly entertaining without taking itself too seriously. Every scene felt fun, fresh, and full of energy, making it a great ride for both longtime fans and newcomers.
If you’re looking for a movie that delivers nonstop laughs, memorable performances, and pure entertainment on the big screen, this is one you won’t want to miss when it hits theaters.

Comedy + thrill + suspense

This summer, comedy legend Jack Black stumbles into the creepiest production of his life in The Barely-Witch Project. Jack plays Jerry Finklebaum, a washed-up filmmaker desperate for a comeback. His genius plan? Reboot The Blair Witch Project… but funnier, spookier, and with “a lot more air guitar solos.”

Jerry drags a ragtag cast and crew into the deep woods of Maryland to film his “masterpiece.” Everything starts fine—until the boom mic operator goes missing, the catering table is replaced with a bubbling cauldron of eye of newt, and someone finds the witch’s Wi-Fi password carved into a tree.

Turns out, there is a real witch (played by an unrecognizable but hilarious Melissa McCarthy), and she’s less “terrifying demon” and more “overbearing Airbnb host with a grudge.” She doesn’t just haunt the crew—she critiques their lighting setups, photobombs scenes, and insists Jack Black “tone it down just a smidge.”

What follows is a wild mashup of spooky hijinks and laugh-out-loud chaos:
	•	Jack Black challenging the witch to a rock-off in the middle of the forest.
	•	A tent collapsing during a “serious” scene because the witch keeps stealing the stakes.
	•	The cast arguing whether it’s “method acting” or just actual terror when they start screaming for real.

With equal parts horror and hilarity, The Barely-Witch Project proves that when you try to reboot a horror classic in the real haunted woods, the scariest thing isn’t the witch—it’s Jack Black improvising at 3 a.m.

Tagline:
“They went into the woods to shoot a horror movie… but the only thing scarier than the witch was the director.”

As of the posting of this trailer, Paul Rudd is 56 years old.  So is Jack Black.  And so is Ice Cube, who was in the original... as are L' Lo and Owen Wilson, also from the original.  All currently 56 years of age, because Anaconda Has A Type.  Jon Voight and Steve Zahn are the outliers, being 86 and 57 respectively.  Although funnily enough, Jon Voight was 57 when Anaconda 1997 released.

The thing with these modern reboots which bugs me, apart from the entire story in the trailer- thanks marketing, is they swap sincerity for sarcasm. The originals had that perfect mix of awe, fear, and adventure, with just enough humor to break tension. Now it’s all quips and over the top CGI, which are definitely budget friendly. Sure, you might laugh once or twice at familiar actors, but the story doesn’t stick — it feels hollow like you'll watch it once and shelve it. That’s why people still rewatch Jumanji or Anaconda. Those movies took themselves seriously, mixed real danger with fantasy, and gave us characters we actually cared about. The new ones, trying too hard to please everyone with comedy, lose the heart that made the originals timeless.”

I'm actually super bummed about this premise. I wasn't wild about the original, because it was just another over exaggerated monster movie that demonized anacondas. So, I was really excited about the first part of the trailer, thinking it could be really funny for them to spoof the original, and loved that they got a very cute (but also realistic) anaconda to film with. Unfortunately, the trailer continued though . . . and instead they're just going to do the exact same as the original - but for "laughs"
Why not just stick with the first idea? It was already a really funny plot, with the low budget, they can still encounter the actual wildlife of the Amazon River, but they can still make a hilarious B-tier horror/comedy film while trying to survive in the wild environment that they have no knowledge of. This cast is full of funny and talented people who could easily do this if they had the guts to do anything original, but I guess that's too much to ask for 🫤

When I was a kid, I watched Anaconda (1997) and Anacondas: The Hunt for the Blood Orchid (2004) and those films scared the hell out of me. They made me respect and fear the snake at the same time. That’s exactly why anaconda became my favorite snake. But after seeing this new trailer, I honestly feel angry, disappointed, and betrayed.

Sony, what the hell is wrong with you? You’ve taken a legendary franchise and turned it into a complete joke. You don’t know how to make a reboot, so why even bother? Just admit it, you’ve destroyed something that was once iconic. You’ve trashed people’s childhood memories for a cheap cash grab.

I refuse to waste my time or money on this embarrassing mess. Seriously, if this is your idea of a reboot, then congratulations, you’ve managed to humiliate not only the franchise but also the fans who grew up loving it.
